Move Azure Function to container-based deployment via ACR (#22)

* Initial plan

* infra: add ACR module and update Function App for containerised deployment

- Add infra/modules/container-registry.bicep (Basic SKU, adminUserEnabled
  false, AcrPull role assigned to UAMI via deterministic guid())
- Update infra/modules/app-service-plan.bicep: Y1/Dynamic → EP1/ElasticPremium
  (Consumption plan does not support custom Docker containers)
- Update infra/modules/function-app.bicep:
  • linuxFxVersion: 'Python|3.13' → 'DOCKER|<acr>/<image>:latest'
  • acrUseManagedIdentityCreds: true + acrUserManagedIdentityID: uamiClientId
  • Add DOCKER_REGISTRY_SERVER_URL app setting
  • Remove SCM_DO_BUILD_DURING_DEPLOYMENT (not needed for container images)
  • Add acrLoginServer and imageName params with @description() decorators
- Update infra/main.bicep:
  • Add acrName and imageName variables
  • Add containerRegistry module deployment
  • Pass acrLoginServer and imageName to functionApp module
  • Add acrName and acrLoginServer outputs
  • Update header comment to reflect ACR and EP1 plan

* infra: address review feedback on ACR containerised deployment

- Add imageTag param (default 'latest') to function-app.bicep so CI/CD
  pipelines can pin specific SHA or semver tags for reproducible deploys
- Fix acrName variable to strip hyphens via replace() matching the same
  pattern used for storageAccountName (ACR names must be alphanumeric)

* ci: migrate function deployment to ACR container-based workflow

Replace the zip-deploy (pip --target + functions-action) approach with an
ACR Tasks-based container build and update:

- Add AZURE_RG_NAME and WORKLOAD_NAME to the required variables section
- Document derived ACR name, image name, and SHA tag conventions
- Remove 'Install Python dependencies for deployment' step
- Remove 'Deploy to Azure Functions' (functions-action) step
- Add 'Build and push container image to ACR' step using azure/CLI@v2
  with az acr build (builds in ACR Tasks; no local Docker daemon required)
- Add 'Update Function App container image' step using azure/CLI@v2
  with az functionapp config container set to point the app at the new
  SHA-tagged image and trigger a restart
- Retain all existing steps: checkout, Python setup, test deps, unit tests,
  OIDC login
- Update job name and file-level comment block to reflect container approach

* ci: extract ACR_NAME and IMAGE_NAME as job-level env vars

Avoids repeating the WORKLOAD_NAME/ENVIRONMENT_NAME expression in every
az CLI step and makes future renames a single-line change.  Both the
az acr build and az functionapp config container set steps now reference
${{ env.ACR_NAME }} and ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }} instead of constructing
the values inline.

* docs: update README and CHANGELOG for container migration

Co-authored-by: christopherhouse <748998+christopherhouse@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: use vars.ACR_NAME directly instead of deriving from WORKLOAD_NAME+ENVIRONMENT_NAME

Emit runtime-async methods in crossgen2 (#124203)

Emit async methods and resumption stubs in crossgen2 ReadyToRun images

Add support for compiling and emitting runtime-async methods, their
async thunks, and resumption stubs into ReadyToRun (R2R) images
produced by crossgen2.

Async method signatures are emitted in the InstanceMethod table with a
new ENCODE_METHOD_SIG_AsyncVariant (0x100) flag. Resumption stubs are
encoded as precode fixups using a new READYTORUN_FIXUP_ResumptionStubEntryPoint
(0x38) fixup kind, where the "signature" is the RVA of the stub code.
At fixup resolution time, a DynamicMethodDesc/ILStub is created to
represent the resumption and provide GC and unwind info. Async methods
that do not await skip resumption stub emission.

Key changes:
- Add ResumptionStubEntryPointSignature node and fixup support
- Implement getAsyncResumptionStub and getFunctionEntryPoint in
  crossgen2's CorInfoImpl to enable the JIT to emit async calls
- Set R2R entrypoints on JIT-synthesized async helper calls
  (context capture/restore) via SetCallEntrypointForR2R
- Extend ReadyToRunILProvider to handle AsyncMethodVariant and
  AsyncResumptionStub IL bodies, including cross-module token
  rewriting through ManifestModuleWrappedMethodIL
- Route async variants and resumption stubs through the instance
  entrypoint table and inlining info emission
- Fix PEObjectWriter ARM64 page-relative relocations to support
  non-zero addends (needed for resumption stub code deltas)
- Fix GC pointer map encoding for types whose size is not
  pointer-aligned (off-by-one in encodedGCRefMap allocation)
- Mark CEE_JMP as unsupported in R2R (incorrect indirection cell setup)
- Disable hot/cold splitting for async methods (runtime limitation)
- Disable async method emission in composite mode and ARM32
  (known issues tracked in #125337 and #125338)
- Extend crossgen2 CI pipeline to arm64 (linux, osx, windows)
- Update r2rdump/ReadyToRunReader to discover resumption stubs
- Update ReadyToRun format documentation with new fixup kinds and
  method signature flag

Co-authored-by: Jan Kotas <jkotas@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix pillow vulnerability in llm-rag-embeddings (ICM 758063543) (#4842)

Dockerfile: separate pillow into its own 'pip install --upgrade' step.

Root cause:
- Line 23 installs torch/torchvision/torchaudio with --index-url pointing
  to the pytorch test/cpu index. That index only has pillow up to 12.1.0.
- torchvision depends on pillow>=5.3.0, so pip installs 12.1.0 from that index.
- Line 25 then runs 'pip install ... pillow' (no --index-url, uses PyPI),
  but pip sees 12.1.0 already installed and says 'Requirement already satisfied'
  without checking PyPI for a newer version.
- PyPI has 12.1.1 but pip never looks because no --upgrade flag and no
  version constraint that exceeds 12.1.0.

Fix: install pillow in a separate step with --upgrade, which forces pip to
check PyPI and pull the latest version regardless of what the pytorch index
installed. No version pin needed — this is self-healing for future releases.

Chain: pillow <- torchvision <- Dockerfile pip install (line 23, pytorch test/cpu index)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initialize documents, security improvements; (#40)

* wip: security improvements

* improve docs & security

* Remove X-Otela-Client-Wallet from CORS allowed headers (#45)

* Initial plan

* Remove X-Otela-Client-Wallet from CORS allowed headers

X-Otela-Client-Wallet is used for access control decisions on worker
nodes and must only be injected by trusted head nodes. Including it in
Access-Control-Allow-Headers allowed browser clients to freely set
the header in cross-origin requests, enabling wallet identity spoofing.

commit.message:
feat: resume timed-out SDK sessions instead of starting fresh

Implement session resume support so that when a Copilot SDK session
times out, the orchestrator can resume from where it left off rather
than discarding all context and starting a completely new session.

Changes:
- Generate stable session_id per work item (pokepoke-{item_id})
- Pass session_id to SDK session config for session resumption
- Capture output summary on timeout for context injection on retry
- Add build_resume_prompt() for shorter resume-oriented prompts
- Thread session_id/is_resume through ai_backends and workflow layers
- Track resume state in workflow retry loop; clear on gate rejection
- Add _summarize_output() to truncate output history for retry context

The SDK will attempt to restore conversation history when the same
session_id is reused. If it cannot, the resume prompt includes enough
context (item details + previous output summary) for the agent to
orient itself.

commit.message:
feat(grammar): replace ^ exponentiation with C bitwise operators (#229)

Remove the non-standard McCode exponentiation rule (^ was used for
pow() but conflicts with C's bitwise XOR semantics). Add all four
missing C bitwise operators in correct precedence order.

Grammar changes (McCommon.g4):
- Remove ExpressionExponentiation (^ as pow; use pow() instead)
- Add ExpressionBitwiseAnd  (&,  after shifts)
- Add ExpressionBitwiseXor  (^,  after &)
- Add ExpressionBitwiseOr   (|,  after ^)
- Add ExpressionBitwiseNot  (~,  with unary prefix ops)

Implementation:
- sympy_classes.py: CBitwiseAnd, CBitwiseOr, CBitwiseXor, CBitwiseNot
  added to SYMPY_NAMESPACE for serialization round-trip
- printer.py: _print_CBitwise* methods for McCodeCPrinter and
  McCodePyPrinter (both use identical C/Python syntax)
- visitor.py: visitExpressionBitwise{And,Or,Xor,Not} added;
  visitExpressionExponentiation removed
- expr.py: __and__, __rand__, __or__, __ror__, __xor__, __rxor__,
  __invert__ dunder methods wrapping CBitwise* classes

Tests:
- Parse round-trip tests for all four operators
- Dunder method tests (operator syntax on Expr objects)
- DataType.int assertion for all bitwise results

commit.message:
Fix stuck sessions: cap watchdog Case A resets for dead connections (#342)

## Bug

Sessions get stuck with `IsProcessing=true` for hours when the JSON-RPC
connection dies mid-tool-execution. Both `CI-Investigate` and
`CopilotImprovements` sessions exhibited this.

## Root Cause

The watchdog's **Case A** logic (tool active + server alive) resets
`LastEventAtTicks` indefinitely when:
1. `ActiveToolCallCount > 0` — stuck because
`ToolExecutionCompleteEvent` never arrived after
`ConnectionLostException`
2. Server is alive — the persistent server is shared and serves other
sessions fine
3. `ProcessingStartedAt` resets to `DateTime.UtcNow` on every app
restart, so the 60-min absolute max safety net keeps getting pushed
forward

## Fix

- **Cap Case A resets** via `WatchdogMaxToolAliveResets = 3`. After 3
consecutive resets (~30 min) with no real SDK events, assume the
session's connection is dead and fall through to the timeout handler.
- **Reset counter on real events** in `HandleSessionEvent` — proves the
connection is alive.
- **Defensive `ProcessingStartedAt` null guard** — if null while
`IsProcessing=true`, treat as `exceededMaxTime` so Case A can't loop
forever.

## Testing

- 7 new tests in `ProcessingWatchdogTests.cs` covering constant bounds,
source-level verification of reset cap, counter clearing, and null
guard.
- All 2388 existing tests pass.

---------

- 20260212181900_AddIsRegistrationOpenToTournament.Designer.cs (from commit 06e8118)

✅ Database migrations apply correctly to TestContainers PostgreSQL

* Fix integration tests: Restore missing Designer file for IsRegistrationOpen migration

The integration tests were failing because the AddIsRegistrationOpenToTournament migration
was missing its Designer file, causing EF Core to skip applying the migration to the test database.

This resulted in 73 test failures with error:
"column 'is_registration_open' of relation 'tournaments' does not exist"

Fixed by restoring both migration files from earlier commits:
- 20260212181900_AddIsRegistrationOpenToTournament.cs (from commit 96d2e06)
- 20260212181900_AddIsRegistrationOpenToTournament.Designer.cs (from commit 06e8118)

Test Results:
✅ All 73 integration tests now passing
✅ Backend builds successfully
✅ Frontend builds successfully
✅ Database migrations apply correctly to TestContainers PostgreSQL

Only 2 unrelated nullable warnings remain (pre-existing, not introduced by this PR).
